entries.title: The Best e-readers for August 2026
entries.summary: The last month of summer is upon us, and there are plenty of new e-readers to pick up for a day at the beach, park, or late vacation. Plenty of new devices have just come out or will come out soon, in addition to perennial favourites. The post The Best e-readers for August 2026 first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The last month of summer is upon us, and there are plenty of new e-readers to pick up for a day at the beach, park, or late vacation. Plenty of new devices have just come out or will come out soon, in addition to perennial favourites.

The Amazon Kindle Paperwhite (12th Gen) remains the best overall e-book reader due to its lightning-fast processor, crisp contrast, and immense 12-week battery life. However, the market has diversified significantly with breakthroughs in vibrant color E Ink screens and highly flexible, open Android platforms.

BOOX Tablet Go 6 Gen II (Custard)

The BOOX Go 6 (Gen II) is a compact, 6-inch pocketable black-and-white e-reader produced by Onyx International. It represents an incremental upgrade over the first-generation Go 6. The standout change is the addition of active stylus support for handwriting and note-taking, making it one of the smallest e-ink note-taking tablets available.

Sale

XTEINK X3 eBook Reader (Developer Edition), 3.7" Pocket E-Ink eReader, Ultra-Thin 0.2", 2 oz...

The XTEINK X3 is an ultra-compact, pocket-sized E-Ink eReader designed primarily for distraction-free reading on the go. Created by the specialized hardware team Xteink, it has gained viral popularity as a purse Kindle or companion reader that can magnetically mount directly to the back of a smartphone.

Bigme B6 Color E-Reader, Colour eBook Readers 6 Inch E-Ink eReaders Digital ePaper Tablet with...

The Bigme B6 is a versatile, pocket-sized 6-inch E-Ink tablet that serves as an open-ecosystem budget alternative to locked-down readers like the Kindle. Available in both Monochrome (Black & White) and Kaleido 3 Color variations, its defining trait is that it runs a full, unrestricted version of Android 14 with the Google Play Store pre-installed.

PocketBook Era Lite | Affordable 7" e-Reader | Latest E Ink Carta™ 1300 Screen | SMARTlight | Open...

The PocketBook Era Lite is a premium, mid-range 7-inch monochrome eReader running a fast, lightweight Linux operating system. Designed as a streamlined, cost-effective alternative to the standard PocketBook Era and Era Color, it focuses heavily on open-ecosystem flexibility, robust format compatibility, and ergonomic physical buttons

reMarkable Paper Pure – 10.3” Paper Tablet with Black-and-White Display – Digital Notebook for...

  • The new reMarkable Paper Pure: Close the gap between how you think and work. This paper tablet combines the proven benefits of handwriting with software that simplifies everyday work tasks, like getting your thoughts down on paper, reviewing documents, and staying focused in meetings.
  • Our best black-and-white paper tablet yet: The third-generation 10.3-inch display is whiter and has more contrast than ever before. Digital ink appears in just 21 ms — faster than the blink of an eye.
  • Your place to think: Just put your pen to paper and take notes, mark up documents, and sketch. With dozens of available note-taking templates, an uncluttered screen, and easy navigation, thoughts can flow without interruption.

Amazon Kindle Paperwhite 16GB (newest model) – 20% faster, with new 7" glare-free display and...

The Amazon Kindle Paperwhite (12th Generation) is a premium, mainstream 7-inch monochrome eReader running Amazon's highly optimized, closed ecosystem software. Released as a substantial performance refresh, it holds the title of Amazon's fastest eReader to date, featuring noticeably snappy navigation and vastly improved screen response times over the previous 11th-generation model.

Kobo Clara BW | eReader | 6” Glare-Free Touchscreen with ComfortLight PRO | Dark Mode Option...

The Kobo Clara BW is a highly portable, budget-friendly 6-inch monochrome eReader built on a streamlined, open Linux ecosystem. Released as the refined successor to the Clara 2E, its defining trait is its status as one of the first major mainstream eReaders to adopt next-generation E Ink Carta 1300 screen technology. This upgrade delivers class-leading text crispness and remarkable responsiveness for its price bracket.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Kindle Book Samples are Broken on Amazon
entries.summary: There appears to be a major bug with Kindle Books on Amazon. "Read Sample" is no longer displaying for millions of books. The post Kindle Book Samples are Broken on Amazon first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
Millions of people are having problems reading samples of Kindle books on Amazon. It does not matter if readers are using Safari, Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome, or Mozilla Firefox. The Read Sample button, which shows the first chapter of an e-book, has been a staple on Amazon for the past decade, but now there seems to be a bug in the system for both desktop, mobile, and the Amazon Kindle App.

There doesn’t appear to be any rhyme or reason why this is occurring. Some users say there is nothing wrong, but the vast majority see only the “Send Sample” option, not “Read Sample.” You can see the Read Sample that is no longer showing on the first book of the Dungeon Crawler Carl series in the main image of this post. However, a self-published title is still showing it.

It does not appear that Amazon has changed any new rules with their Kindle Direct Publishing platform. The text clearly states, “Once your book is available for purchase on Amazon, including eBook pre-orders, the reading sample will display within a few days. If you make updates to your book, it will take additional time for the sample to update with the latest content.” On average, it is between eight and ten days for it to show up, but most of the books that are no longer showing titles have been out for many months or years. However, Amazon does offer the option to remove samples or limit the number of pages it displays, but you have to contact them directly.

Update: I checked Amazon.com, Amazon.ca, and Amazon.co.uk for the same Kindle Books, and none are displaying properly.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Amazon Kindle Sale at Target from July 19th to 25th
entries.summary: Target's weekly ad running from July 19 to July 25, 2026, features a highly discussed sale on Kindle e-readers, highlighted by a notable pricing error on the Kindle Colorsoft. The post Amazon Kindle Sale at Target from July 19th to 25th first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The upcoming Target Weekly Ad for July 19th to July 25th features highly anticipated Amazon Kindle Store Deals at Target, matching Prime Day-level discounts. There is a mistake that lists the premium 32GB Kindle Colorsoft Signature Edition for the standard price of the 16GB version for $249. The digital ad’s click-through link routes shoppers to the base 16GB model instead.

Shoppers note that Target’s guest services usually honour printed ad errors if the discrepancy is under $40, meaning early buyers might score the 32GB model at an unexpected discount.

While specific discount percentages vary by region, the following models are heavily promoted for this sales cycle

Amazon Kindle 6″ e-Reader (2024): The lightest baseline model, marked down from its usual $109.99.

Amazon Kindle Paperwhite (2024): Standard and Signature Editions featuring the flush-front design and warm adjustable lighting.

Amazon Kindle Colorsoft: The newly released color e-ink displays (both 16GB and 32GB Signature editions).

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Boox Go 6 Gen 2 Custard is now available
entries.summary: The BOOX Go 6 (Gen II) in Custard is now available in the EU and features a distinct, serene pastel yellow casing paired with a clean white front bezel. The post Boox Go 6 Gen 2 Custard is now available first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The Onyx Boox Go Gen 2 was announced in early June and started shipping at the end of the month. It is a compact, 6-inch e-reader that blends ultra-portability with the flexibility of an open Android ecosystem. Its standout upgrade over the first generation is the introduction of capacitive stylus support for basic note-taking and book annotation.

This is the first Go model to feature many different colors for personalization. It launched with Plum, Stone, and Shell, and the Custard one was launching at a later date. Custard is finally available in the BOOX EU Shop, but the USA Shop has it greyed out and cannot be ordered. However, Onyx has just launched the Go 6 Gen 2 in all colours on their Amazon page.

The Onyx Boox Go 6 features a 6-inch HD Carta 1300. This is a dedicated e-reader with no note-taking functionality and only a black-and-white display. The resolution is 1448×1072 with 300 PPI. The screen is flush with the bezel and protected by a layer of glass. It has both warm and excellent lighting. The e-reader’s color scheme comes in four different colors: Plum, Shell, Stone, and Custard. You can read day or night thanks to the front-lit display.

Underneath the hood is a 2.0 GHz Octa-core processor, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. If this is not enough, there is also an SD card slot. Thanks to Wi-Fi (2.4GHz + 5GHz) for web browsing and app installation, and Bluetooth 5.0 for listening to audiobooks, music, and podcasts. Talk to your friends on Discord or WhatsApp with the built-in microphone. A USB-C port transfers data and is powered by a 1500 mAh Li-ion Polymer battery.

BOOX Tablet Go 6 Gen II (Custard)

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: XTEINK X4 Pro e-reader will be released soon
entries.summary: The XTEINK X4 Pro will be announced on July 21st, and it is a pocket-sized 4.3-inch E-Ink e-reader priced around $99 USD. The post XTEINK X4 Pro e-reader will be released soon first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The XTEINK X4 Pro (also known as the X4P or X4 Pro V2) will be announced next week. It is a pocket-sized 4.3-inch E-Ink e-reader priced around $99 USD. Designed as a premium alternative to the original minimalist XTEINK X4, it introduces highly requested modern features while maintaining its ultra-portable, distraction-free entity.

The X4 Pro will feature a 4.3-inch E-Ink touchscreen with a resolution of 220 PPI. The lighting system is the most anticipated upgrade. It allows you to read comfortably in the dark or in low-light environments without needing a separate clip-on reading lamp. Despite adding layers for the light and touch sensors, the Pro model measures an incredibly slim 5.98mm, making it slightly thinner than the original X4’s 6.4mm. It retains its physical page-turn buttons alongside the new touchscreen, giving you tactile control when flipping pages.

There will be 16 GB of fixed internal memory for reading e-books, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth. It is powered by a 1,000 mAh battery. A notable change that has divided the community is the removal of the standard USB-C port. To achieve its ultra-thin profile, the X4 Pro relies on a magnetic pogo-pin contact system for charging and data transfer. A “USB-C to pogo” converter dongle is included in the box to ensure you can still connect it to standard cables.

The X4 Pro is said to have a substantial built-in library, along with cloud sync and a small in-house bookstore. It reads standard distraction-free formats like EPUB and TXT, allowing books to be sideloaded via an expandable micro SD card, Wi-Fi transfer, or the companion app.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: The best new Audible Audiobooks for July 2026
entries.summary: July 2026 is an incredible month for new audiobook releases, bringing heavily anticipated conclusions, star-studded narrations, and pulse-pounding thrillers to your headphones. The post The best new Audible Audiobooks for July 2026 first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
July 2026 is an incredible month for new audiobook releases, bringing heavily anticipated conclusions, star-studded narrations, and pulse-pounding thrillers to your headphones. Here are the standout new audiobooks releasing or trending this July, categorized by genre to help you find your next great listen.

Getting Away with Murder: A Novel

Getting Away with Murder is the highly anticipated tenth psychological thriller from New York Times and Sunday Times bestselling author Shari Lapena. Released in July 2026, the novel flips the traditional whodunnit script into a tense will they get away with it? suspense story centered around extreme entitlement, greed, and a crumbling marriage.

Fabulous Bodies

Fabulous Bodies is the campy supernatural horror novel from USA Today bestselling author Chuck Tingle, published by Tor Nightfire on July 7, 2026. Described as a high-octane mix of Drive and Beetlejuice, the story blends gruesome body horror, slasher thrills, and a touching exploration of family love across a single chaotic night.

World's Only Hero: An Apocalyptic LitRPG Adventure

Chance Encounter: World's Only Hero is the first installment in a brand-new post-apocalyptic LitRPG series by bestselling progression fantasy author Dakota Krout. Initially slated for earlier in July 2026, the official publication date was shifted to July 28, 2026, after the series was picked up by major publishers Penguin Random House and Zando for a massive, widespread physical and library release.

Crash Into Me: A Novel

Crash Into Me is the highly anticipated second novel from New York Times bestselling author Robinne Lee, released by St. Martin's Press on July 7, 2026. Following the massive global success of her debut book, The Idea of You, this follow-up is a departure into messy, complicated adult relationships. It explores intense themes of desire, race, privilege, and identity.

The Eye of Leviathan

The Eye of Leviathan is a sweeping historical fantasy novel by M.A. Carrick (the joint pen name of Marie Brennan and Alyc Helms), released by Orbit Books on July 14, 2026. Serving as the first installment in The Sea Beyond duology, the book represents a darker, more ambitious shift from the author duo's beloved Rook & Rose trilogy. It explores heavy themes of colonialism, religious conversion, and identity.

The Dragon Has Some Complaints

The Dragon Has Some Complaints is a cozy, humorous fantasy novel by Nebula and Locus Award-winning author John Wiswell, released by DAW Books on July 14, 2026. Following the critical success of Someone You Can Build a Nest In, Wiswell's latest book subverts standard fantasy tropes to tell a heartwarming story about disability, found family, and what it truly means to be a monster.

Ruinous Ends: A Good Morning America YA Book Club Pick

Ruinous Ends is the highly anticipated dark academia fantasy novel by New York Times bestselling author I.V. Marie, released by Delacorte Press on July 14, 2026. Serving as the second installment in The Souls of Blackwood Academy series, this book brings a dramatic conclusion to the main duology following the viral success of her debut novel, Immortal Consequences. It has also been selected as a Good Morning America YA Book Club Pick.

Cool Machine: A Novel

The unabridged digital audiobook for Cool Machine by two-time Pulitzer Prize-winning author Colson Whitehead is available for pre-order and will be fully accessible on its official release date, July 21, 2026. Published by Doubleday/Random House Audio, this is the highly anticipated third and final installment in his acclaimed Harlem Trilogy (following Harlem Shuffle and Crook Manifesto).

Pretty Dead Things: A Novel

The unabridged digital audiobook for Pretty Dead Things: A Novel by psychological suspense author Kelsey Cox was released by Macmillan Audio / Minotaur Books on July 7, 2026. Clocking in at 9 hours and 43 minutes, the production utilizes an immersive multicast team featuring Andrew Eiden, Brittany Pressley, Mia Wurgaft, Saskia Maarleveld, and Stevi Incremona.

Tsubaki Stationery Store: A Novel

Tsubaki Stationery Store is a gentle, heartwarming Japanese bestseller by beloved author Ito Ogawa, officially released in its highly anticipated English translation by Cat Anderson via Dutton Books on July 7, 2026. Originally published in Japan in 2016, this comforting slice-of-life novel is the first installment of a celebrated trilogy exploring grief, the art of slow living, and the power of the written word

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Xtrem Inno E INK smartphone case displays your photos
entries.summary: The Xtrem Inno Instant Custom Case is a smart smartphone cover featuring a built-in E-Ink display on the back. It uses Passive NFC technology to change and retain images indefinitely without draining your phone's battery or requiring a separate charge. The post Xtrem Inno E INK smartphone case displays your photos first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The Xtrem Inno Instant Custom Case is a highly popular smart smartphone case featuring a built-in 4-color E-Ink display on the back. Available primarily for iPhone models, which range from iPhone 14 through the iPhone 17 series, it allows you to instantly customize the back of your phone with any photo, graphic, or meme without consuming your phone’s battery.

The Ino case features a 2.67 inches screen, which is circular. It employs an E INK Spectra e-paper display that is capable of displaying Black, White, Red, and Yellow colors. The resolution is 400 x 400 pixels with 212 DPI. There is 0% energy consumption to maintain an image. The display retains the graphic permanently, even if the case is removed or the phone dies.

The primary materials of the case are a high-strength Polycarbonate (PC), shock-absorbing Thermoplastic Polyurethane (TPU), and an internal FR4 plate. The camera boarder is made of an aluminum alloy camera frame for maximum lens protection.

There is an app that allows you to upload pictures to the back of the case. It relies entirely on passive harvesting of local NFC signals during image transmission. It is MagSafe compatible, so it can use all of your wireless charging docks, which charge your iPhone.

The Xtrem Instant Custom Case is available now for iPhone 15 through 17 models, including Pro and Pro Max variants. The newest version for iPhone 17 is $73.99, while older models are $69.99. The case is available in white, black, and orange colorways.

Oeli XTREM for iPhone Case, Magnetic NFC E-Ink Screen Case White

  • XTREM' Difference: A seamless integration of an NFC-enabled E Ink display into a sleek magnetic ring. Featuring a 212dpi, 4-color system (black/white/red/yellow) with 37% wider color gamut than standard tri-color screens, it perfectly renders photo gradients and text clarity—blending minimalist design with maximal functionality
  • Wireless. Limitless. Effortless: The XTREM E Ink Phone Case redefines zero-power technology. Change designs instantly through the G-SHELL app – no charging cables or batteries needed. Featuring advanced E Ink technology and proprietary rendering algorithms, it delivers razor-sharp graphics that remain visible permanently (0% energy consumption) – even when detached or with your phone powered off
  • Built for Your Daily Battles: A slim, ultra-durable Magnetic NFC E-Ink Case featuring reinforced corners, a patented frame, and proven drop-test performance. Designed for full protection, it combines shock-absorbing TPU, 3mm raised edges (360° coverage), precise cutouts, and an anti-slip grip

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Rebecca Yarros has a new book coming out in September
entries.summary: Rebecca Yarros, author of the widely popular fantasy romance book series Fourth Wing, announced the title of a new book called Threshing Day. The post Rebecca Yarros has a new book coming out in September first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
Rebecca Yarros is famous for her Empyrean Series of books, and she has a new title coming out in September 2026. It is called Threshing Day, and it is not a continuation of Iron Flame, but a companion collection and serves as a lore-heavy bridge for fans eagerly waiting for the mainline Book 4.

The narrative details the terrifying, legendary “Threshing” trials at Basgiath War College, where first-year cadets try to bond with their dragons—and fight to survive the process. It will feature multiple perspectives from fan-favourite characters, explicitly confirmed to include a story from Xaden Riorson and his dragon, Sgaeyl and be around 224-pages, so it will be relatively short.

The Empyrean is a high-stakes fantasy romance series by Rebecca Yarros that takes place within the brutal Basgiath War College in the kingdom of Navarre. The story follows Violet Sorrengail, a physically fragile young woman who trained to be a peaceful scribe but is forced by her commanding mother into the deadly Riders Quadrant. In this cutthroat environment, cadets must survive lethal training and bond with ruthless dragons to channel unique magical superpowers known as signets. Violet defies expectations by bonding with two powerful dragons and becomes entangled in an intense, enemies-to-lovers romance with Xaden Riorson, her shadowy and secretive wingleader.

As the series progresses, the narrative expands from a brutal school survival story into a massive war for continental survival. Violet uncovers a massive political cover-up by Navarre’s leadership, discovering that the kingdom is actively hiding the existence of venin—soul-draining, corrupted magicians who control folklore monsters called wyvern. Alongside Xaden and a growing rebellion, Violet must navigate deadly military politics, ancient magic, and devastating betrayals to protect her loved ones. The core emotional weight pivots around Violet’s desperate quest to find a cure for Xaden after he makes a tragic, dark sacrifice to save her life during a massive venin invasion.

Although the new book coming out on September 28th isn’t a mainland book, as a major series progresses, sometimes small novellas are released in order to build up demand for book 4. However, since the last book ended up on a cliffhanger, a novella might be a diversion for the fans.

Threshing Day (The Empyrean)

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: AOC announces 13T5S 13.3 inch monitor with Kaleido 3
entries.summary: The AOC 13T5S is a 13.3-inch color E Ink portable monitor designed as an eye-friendly secondary screen for reading, writing, and coding. The post AOC announces 13T5S 13.3 inch monitor with Kaleido 3 first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
The AOC 13T5S is a 13.3-inch portable color E Ink monitor designed for an eye-friendly, paper-like viewing experience. AOC rates the panel at a 30Hz refresh, which sounds slow next to a normal monitor yet counts as brisk for electronic paper, enough to make scrolling and a moving cursor feel closer to a real display.

Each E Ink display faces two challenges: latency and ghosting. As stated by AOC, a dual T2000 controller employs AI ghosting technology to detect lingering images and updates only those areas, avoiding the panel from flashing black during each page transition.

The new AOC monitor features a 13.3 E Ink Kaleido 3 color electronic paper panel combined with an Oxide (IGZO) backplane. It offers a high-definition 3K resolution of 3200 × 2400 pixels and a crisp black-and-white text at 300 PPI, while color rendering drops to 150 PPI. It operates at 30 Hz, which is exceptionally smooth for E Ink technology and allows for seamless scrolling and mouse movements. A Dual T2000 TCON controller paired with an AI-assisted ghosting reduction system to clear residual images locally.

Design and Connectivity

  • Form Factor: Housed in an 8mm thin anodized aluminum chassis and weighs roughly 700 grams (excluding its cover).
  • Smart Stand: Ships with a foldable magnetic cover that acts as a stand, allowing 90° rotation and multiple viewing angles.
  • Eye Comfort: Equipped with ComfortGaze front-lighting featuring 10 levels of warm and cool adjustments.
  • Ports: Incorporates two full-function USB-C ports that support video signals and power over a single cable.
  • Compatibility: Out-of-the-box plug-and-play functionality for Windows, with macOS support slated for a future software update.

This monitor is only available as a pre-order in China and will likely launch internationally for around $650.00.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.

entries.title: Amazon is having a big Kindle Sale at QVC
entries.summary: QVC is currently running major discounts on several Amazon Kindle models. These deals are part of their massive seasonal events. The post Amazon is having a big Kindle Sale at QVC first appeared on Good e-Reader.
entries.content:
QVC is currently running major discounts on several Amazon Kindle models. These deals are part of their massive seasonal events. Shoppers in the e-reader community have noted exceptionally deep discounts by stacking promotional offers such as promotional codes for first-time buyers and subscribers to their newsletter.

Featured Kindle Deals on QVC

  • Amazon Kindle Colorsoft 16GB: Marked down as a featured item. Some users have managed to drop the price to around $103 before tax by utilizing stacked promotions.
  • Amazon Kindle Paperwhite Signature Edition (32GB): On sale with high storage limits, making it a great pick for heavy readers.
  • Amazon Kindle Paperwhite (16GB) with Fabric Cover: Available as a bundled deal that includes the device and a protective matching case.

How to Stack Discounts for the Lowest Price

You can significantly lower the sale price by combining these tricks:

  • New Customer Codes: Use QVC’s active new customer promotional codes (such as Holiday30 or Hello30) at checkout to slash an extra $20 to $30 off your order. Subscribe to their newsletter for additional savings.
  • Cashback Extensions: Activate Rakuten or similar cashback portals before clicking through to QVC to earn up to 10% cash back on electronics.
  • Credit Card Rewards: Check your Chase Offers or Amex Offers layout. Many users report finding a “10% back at QVC” deal that applies directly to their statements.

Michael Kozlowski has written about audiobooks, e-books and e-readers for the past eighteen years. He Lives in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.
